channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: Hackers Claim GTA 6 Developer Rockstar Games is Once Again Breached
item.content:encoded:
A hacker group has claimed to have breached GTA 6 developer Rockstar Games and set a ransom demand for the developers to pay by April 14.

The group, which goes by the name ‘ShinyHunters’ has added Rockstar Games to its dark web leak website, claiming that the studio has now been breached.

According to the group, the group breached the studio via Anodot, a SaaS cloud-cost monitoring tool that Rockstar uses. The group claims to have gained access to Rockstar Games’ financial data, player spending habits, marketing timelines, and contracts with outsourcing companies.

If the ransom is paid by the group’s April 14 deadline, then all of the code could be leaked online.

So far, there’s been no evidence or claims from the hacker group that player passwords or data have been breached, but it still might be worth changing your passwords and enabling 2FA on your Rockstar accounts if you haven’t already.

Another GTA 6 Hack

For those in the loop, this isn’t the first time that GTA 6 and Rockstar Games have been hacked. In 2022, one hacker breached Rockstar Games in one of the industry’s biggest hacks, leaking over 90 gameplay videos of in-development GTA 6 footage.

During a court hearing following the arrest of the hacker, prosecutor Kevin Barry referred to the GTA 6 leaks during the hearing, saying,

“This material was highly confidential as the game is a billion-dollar franchise and the latest installment is hotly anticipated. He published some of the stolen footage taken during the hack on online forums and blackmailed Rockstar Games that he would release the precious source code which forms the building blocks of the game unless they contacted him – presumably to discuss ransom payment.”

As of the time of writing, Rockstar Games and Take-Two Interactive, the publisher of GTA 6 have yet to make a comment. Should one be received, we will update the article.

GTA 6 is currently scheduled to release on November 19, 2026, on the PlayStation 5 and Xbox Series S|X. No official release date for PC has been provided yet.

Do you think the recent GTA 6 hack could see the game delayed again? Let us know your thoughts on the debate on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the news about the new Metro 4 leak

Tom Henderson is Insider Gaming's Co-Founder and Editor-In-Chief. When he's not running one of the industry's leading independent video game websites, he's probably playing an FPS like Call of Duty,…

More from Tom Henderson

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android



https://insider-gaming.com/gta-6-hacked-again/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Escape from Tarkov Survey Mentions Adding Prestige to PvE Platform
item.content:encoded:
If there’s one thing many PvE-focused Escape from Tarkov players feel alienates them from the base game, it’s the lack of Prestige mechanics across the PvE platform. In December 2024, Battlestate Games introduced the Prestige system, but by today, it’s still only available for those playing PvP, and with each passing day, PvE becomes more popular.

The latest Escape from Tarkov survey, which has some brilliant rewards, talks about potentially adding Prestige to PvE, along with asking all manner of questions about the difficulty, nature, and rewards associated with Prestige in PvP.

Should Tarkov’s Prestige System Undergo Huge Changes?

Battlestate Games is probing the Escape from Tarkov player base about the Prestige system in Escape from Tarkov. As you can imagine, Prestige allows players to reset their profile when they hit a pre-determined bunch of requirements, going up a tier, securing permanent rewards, and starting all over again.

It’s an aspirational thing that comes with a slew of challenging tick boxes, and it’s still expanding, but it’s seen as one of the game’s ultimate challenges by the player base.

It’s not without issues, of course. One of the main problems with Prestige is that the mechanic doesn’t exist on the PvE platform, which is fast becoming more popular as it aligns closer with the traditional PvP-focused offering.

The stigma associated with being a PvE main is dissipating as it becomes more widely accepted.

That’s why PvE players perked up when they saw one of the questions in the most recent Escape from Tarkov survey, designed to probe users and get their thoughts about Prestige:

The other questions were around the difficulty of hitting a new Prestige tier, the value of the rewards that follow, and what should constitute a requirement for making it to the next Prestige level. There was also a question about how long it should take to climb the ladder, with the minimum investment being 100 hours.

It’s also worth noting that the rewards for completing this survey are tasty, giving players a relatively rare item, the UHF RFID reader. It’s not hugely valuable (around 65,000 on the Flea), but you’ll need one, alongside a VPX module, for Import, a quest that unlocks at level 35.

Here’s the full spread of rewards for this new Escape from Tarkov survey:

Do you think PvE players should be allowed to hit Prestige levels, just like those playing PvP? Let us know your thoughts on the debate on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the news about the new Metro 4 leak

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant has been gaming for 30+ years and in the industry for 10+. You'll probably find him playing a post-apocalyptic game or an extraction shooter somewhere.

More from Grant Taylor-Hill

MOBILE APP

item.title: Metro 4 Leak Reportedly Unveils Canceled Game Led by Hunter
item.content:encoded:
The Metro space has been blowing up in the last day, thanks to two huge stories. First, it was claimed that Metro 4 (not the game’s confirmed title) will be revealed next week, putting an end to six years of assumptions and guesses from the community. Then, claims surfaced online that a massive 120 GB leak had uncovered a canned version of Metro 4, shelved four years ago.

Metro fans busied themselves digging through the reported leak, which was said to have contained more than 20 zipped files of content, including maps, dialogue, character models, and more.

In a post on Reddit, a user shared images of what was claimed to be Metro 4, taken from a version of the game that was canned by 4A Games in 2022. Per the post:

The game was about Hunter and took place from Jan 2033 to Dec 2034. It was fully open world, had a full crafting system with alt ammo types and more. You had an upgradeable NBC suit, and Khan and Bourbon were main characters too. There were rumours in different stations you could disprove such as alcohol numbing radiation and females wearing NBC suits shooting quicker than males.

The subreddit’s moderation team locked down the thread because it infringed on a rule preventing the sharing of pirated software, cracks, and torrents, as some users set about sharing links to the leaked content.

As the conversation unravelled, those digging into the alleged leak uncovered more ‘information’:

  • The game would have been open across both the metro tunnels and the ‘overworld’
  • Artyom would not have been a playable character, but would instead have been retired to the background of the universe
  • It would have followed the path of the book, Metro 2034, fully reviving Hunter’s character
  • Multiple factions and stations would have been discoverable, including Nazi and Red stations
  • The bullet-based economy had been altered to a more barter-based economy

As with any leak of this nature, it’s important to take every aspect with a pinch of salt, especially as the Discord server sharing the content seems to have been locked down to prevent further sharing.

What we do know is that 4A Games has been developing Metro 4 (name TBC) since at least 2020, when the project was announced. That came just a year after the launch of Metro Exodus. Then, in 2023, Insider Gaming exclusively revealed that a fully playable version of Metro 4 was available, which would land after this version of the game was canned.

It’s not a huge stretch to assume that this leak is legitimate, especially as several screenshots have surfaced online and across social media, but it’s still important to stress that, if accurate, it was scrapped years ago.

It was recently claimed that Metro 4 should be revealed next week, so either way, we could have the truth of it soon enough.

Are you looking forward to the next Metro game? Let us know your thoughts on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the news that Phantom Blade Zero’s developer has spoken against AI in gaming

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant has been gaming for 30+ years and in the industry for 10+. You'll probably find him playing a post-apocalyptic game or an extraction shooter somewhere.

More from Grant Taylor-Hill

item.title: Phantom Blade Zero Dev Speaks Out Against AI in Gaming
item.content:encoded:
In a lengthy post on social media, the team at S-GAME, the studio behind the upcoming Phantom Blade Zero, spoke out against the use of AI technology in game development. The post stressed that, at every opportunity, S-GAME has hired real people to work on the game’s artistic elements, avoiding the use of AI technology as much as possible.

In their words, they think that ‘human artistry is not merely a means for creating value; it is the value itself.’

S-GAME Blocks AI Involvement in Phantom Blade Zero

The recent post on social media from S-GAME confirmed that Phantom Blade Zero is ‘in the intense, final stages of development.’ It was then stated very clearly that the accelerating timeline is not forcing S-GAME to resort to AI technology to get things done quicker, even if that’s the trend in today’s games industry.

Instead, everything from the character models, weapons, in-game maps, and voiceover segments is being (and always has been) painstakingly created with traditional human input, pushing firmly away from artificial intelligence-based solutions.

The team wrote:

We are fully aware that a profound technological revolution is unfolding around us. However, to this day, every single piece of content in our game has been crafted by the hands of real artists. We will not use AI visual tech that could alter our artists’ original creative intent.

With that being said, the game’s weapons have been hand-drawn based on traditional Chinese weaponry. The combat mechanics and move sets have been ‘motion-captured by over twenty highly experienced martial artists.’ The maps that guide players through the game have been hand-drawn using ‘Chinese brushes and Xuan paper.’

They wrote:

S-GAME didn’t just hire a group of developers to make a game; rather, in our pursuit of continuously building an exceptional, passionate team, we decided to make a game that everyone here could be deeply proud of.

Many of Phantom Blade Zero’s prospective fans have found this clarity and transparency refreshing in an age where so many studios are resorting to artificial solutions to iterate faster and more cheaply.

Are you happy that S-GAME is kicking back against AI in games? Let us know your thoughts on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the news about Crimson Desert’s latest patch

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant has been gaming for 30+ years and in the industry for 10+. You'll probably find him playing a post-apocalyptic game or an extraction shooter somewhere.

item.title: NYT Connections Hints Today (#1035)—Answers for Saturday, April 11
item.content:encoded:
It’s finally the weekend, but before you can relax (or do whatever else you have planned), you need to dominate today’s NYT Connections puzzle. Fortunately, I’m here with the best NYT Connections hints and solutions you’ll find anywhere. If you’re stuck on today’s challenge or want the easiest ride possible, simply scroll down and get the answers.

How to Play NYT Connections

In June 2023, NYT Connections emerged as yet another game in the ever-growing lineup of the New York Times titles. This title soared immediately, and it’s easy to see why. As a free-to-play, accessible New York Times game, NYT Connections is available worldwide and can be played on desktop devices or in a mobile app.

But how do you play NYT Connections?

The NYT team will provide you with a grid of 16 words once a day. The only task left up to you is to sort those words into four groups of four by finding their commonalities. Every NYT Connections puzzle has just one solution, and some words can have double meanings, so it can sometimes be tricky to figure out the answers.

It’s also important to stress that the words are color-coded: yellow, blue, green, and purple. That’s in order of difficulty, from least to most.

What’s critical here is that you get four guesses every day, and if you use those, your NYT Connections answers for today will be out of reach, as it’ll be game over.

Today’s NYT Connections Hints (April 11)

Here are the words from today’s NYT Connections puzzle:

  • ANGEL
  • SNOWFLAKE
  • JACK
  • SCREWDRIVER
  • BOMBAY
  • STRUT
  • PATRON
  • BEAM
  • CHAMPION
  • CHELSEA
  • COLUMN
  • ICE SCAPER
  • SPARE TIRE
  • BRACE
  • SPONSOR
  • JUMPER CABLES

And here are the hints I can offer for today’s NYT Connections:

  • Yellow – Things you might keep in your car
  • Green – Words that describe people who donate money to things
  • Blue – Words that describe things that support buildings and structures
  • Purple – Words that end with terms for bodies of water

Here’s the final warning before we get to the NYT Connections answers for today, April 11:

Are you sure you want to scroll further?

Very well!

NYT Connections Answers Explained for Today, April 11 (#1035)

If you want to jump straight to the answers for today’s NYT Connections puzzle, I’ve got you covered:

  • Yellow – ICE SCRAPER, JACK, JUMPER CABLES, SPARE TIRE
  • Green – ANGEL, CHAMPION, PATRON, SPONSOR
  • Blue – BEAM, BRACE, COLUMN, STRUT
  • Purple – BOMBAY, CHELSEA, SCREWDRIVER, SNOWFLAKE

When Does NYT Connections Reset?

That’s today’s puzzle, but if you want the next one, the game resets daily at midnight local time (that’s your local time). You’ll have 24 hours to complete the puzzle when midnight ticks over in your timezone.

How did you do? Did you get a perfect run? Let me know what your streak looks like on the Insider Gaming Discord server

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant has been gaming for 30+ years and in the industry for 10+. You'll probably find him playing a post-apocalyptic game or an extraction shooter somewhere.

More from Grant Taylor-Hill

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android

item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Pete Hines Left Bethesda Because It Was Getting ‘Damaged, Mistreated, Abused’
item.content:encoded:
In October 2023, Pete Hines stepped away from Bethesda Game Studios after almost a quarter of a century at the company. In a lengthy statement, the industry veteran explained it wasn’t a decision he made quickly, and he felt that, with Starfield having just launched, the time was right to depart and move on to whatever would come next.

However, it seems that the truth has been bubbling within Hines ever since. In a recent interview, the recognizable former developer was remarkably transparent about why he left Bethesda and what place the company has under the Microsoft banner.

‘My Mental Health was so Deplorable’

In an interview with Kirk McKeand on the new Firezide Chat series, Pete Hines went in about his time at Bethesda Game Studios, revealing intimate and pent-up thoughts about why he left the company.

He suggested he didn’t exactly want to leave, as it was extremely difficult walking away from the team, but what the company had become under Microsoft simply didn’t interest him anymore:

I was staying there because this place still needs me. I just hit a point of yes, it needs me, and I am powerless to do what I think needs to be done to run this place properly, to protect these people, to maintain what we worked so hard to create, which is an incredibly efficient, well run video game developer and publisher.

When I was unable to do what I thought my job should involve in continuing to have that place be, you know, if not the most efficient publisher in the game industry, it was way the f*** up there.

And when I couldn’t protect it, and I saw how it was getting damaged and broken apart and frankly mistreated, abused, whatever word you want to use, I said I am not going to sit here and watch this happen right in front of me.

He then explained that Todd Howard, his friend and colleague for more than 20 years, was the only one who knew he was leaving the company for such a long time. He stressed that, with each new Starfield delay, his departure was pushed back several months, and that weighed heavily on his mental health.

I think I’ve done everything I can do. This is not when I wanted it to end or how I wanted it to end, but that’s not really up to me. And at a certain point, truthfully, my mental health was so deplorable that I just said I cannot.

Under Microsoft, Pete Hines said that Bethesda Game Studios ‘is not authentic and is not genuine,’ following that up with a bold statement: ‘And that shouldn’t be a surprise to you.’

Microsoft acquired ZeniMax and Bethesda Game Studios, amongst other firms, in a massive 2021 takeover. The move has never sat well with some veterans, and that includes Pete Hines, who departed the company two years after the acquisition.

Are you surprised to learn why Pete Hines left Bethesda Game Studios? Let us know your thoughts on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the news that a new Metro game could be revealed soon

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant has been gaming for 30+ years and in the industry for 10+. You'll probably find him playing a post-apocalyptic game or an extraction shooter somewhere.

More from Grant Taylor-Hill

item.title: NYT Strands #769 Hints and Answers for Today, April 11, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Strands is a daily word search puzzle game from The New York Times. It is a relatively new game, launched in 2024, in which players seek a common theme among words in a 6×8 letter grid.

Whether it’s your first time playing or you’re a Strands veteran, today’s tips and strategies can help you find words faster. So, if you’ve found yourself stuck with today’s Strands, don’t worry, below you’ll find some helpful hints and even the answers for Strands Game #769 for Thursday, April 11, 2026

To play today’s Strands, head to the game’s website.

IMPORTANT: The following information includes spoilers on today’s edition of Strands. If you don’t want the answer given to you, stop scrolling.

How To Play Strands

Strands is a daily word search that challenges you to find words with a common theme. There’s also a Spanagram that reveals the deeper theme behind the words.

  • Words that are three letters and under do not count.
  • Words that reveal the correct theme appear in blue.
  • The Spanagram turns yellow when found.
  • Finding three words that don’t fit the theme will reveal a hint to help you find the proper words.

The goal is to string together as many words as possible. Every letter on the grid gets used! Daily puzzles keep players coming back for more, making it a fun brain exercise and a popular online trend.

Strands can only be played on the New York Times website or on the NYT Games app.

Strands Hints For Today, April 11, 2026

The Strands’ answers to the theme of Game #769 are all related to different types of hats.

General Strands Hints For April 11

Without giving today’s Strands answers completely away, here are a few helpful hints for today to get you started:

  • Hint 1: M’lady.
  • Hint 2: Worn by residents of Tostarena in Super Mario Odyssey.
  • Hint 3: The Spanagram is also the name of a popular car TV series.

Use these clues to narrow down your searches and increase your chances of solving Strands #769 for April 11, 2026.

Words to Help You Find Strand Hints for April 11, 2026

If you want help getting started without having today’s Strands answer given to you, there are a few starter words worth trying that will help you unlock some in-game hints. A few starter words that may help are:

  • SOME
  • FEAR
  • ROTO
  • PAGE
  • BAGEL

Today’s Strands Answer For Game #769 On April 11, 2026

If you’ve tried everything, including using the above hints, and are still stuck, you can find the answers to today’s Strands below. Check out the answers for past puzzles here.

  • DERBY
  • SOMBRERO
  • BOWLER
  • BERET
  • FEDORA
  • BEANIE
  • TOQUE
  • SPANAGRAM: TOPGEAR

If you solved today’s Strands without our answers, congratulations! If not, take note of the best tips above for tomorrow’s puzzle, and check back daily for more hints.

When Is The Next Strands Game After #769?

All New York Times games are updated daily at 12 am or Midnight in your respective time zone. Information about the next game is often available in the hours leading up to it, giving players a heads-up. The game on April 12 will be Strands Game #770.

What Is Strands?

Strands is a daily word search puzzle game created by The New York Times. Launched in 2024, Strands challenges players to discover a common theme among words in a 6×8 letter grid. The game’s original concept was developed by Juliette Seive, a research director on The New York Times Games team, with the aim of replicating Wordle’s success. Tracy Bennett serves as the puzzle editor for Strands, overseeing the creation and curation of daily puzzles since its launch.

For even more Insider Gaming delivered straight to your inbox, sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android



https://insider-gaming.com/nyt-strands-769-hints-and-answers-for-today-april-11-2026/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: NYT Strands #768 Hints and Answers for Today, April 10, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Strands is a daily word search puzzle game from The New York Times. It is a relatively new game, launched in 2024, in which players seek a common theme among words in a 6×8 letter grid.

Whether it’s your first time playing or you’re a Strands veteran, today’s tips and strategies can help you find words faster. So, if you’ve found yourself stuck with today’s Strands, don’t worry, below you’ll find some helpful hints and even the answers for Strands Game #768 for Friday, April 10, 2026

To play today’s Strands, head to the game’s website.

IMPORTANT: The following information includes spoilers on today’s edition of Strands. If you don’t want the answer given to you, stop scrolling.

How To Play Strands

Strands is a daily word search that challenges you to find words with a common theme. There’s also a Spanagram that reveals the deeper theme behind the words.

  • Words that are three letters and under do not count.
  • Words that reveal the correct theme appear in blue.
  • The Spanagram turns yellow when found.
  • Finding three words that don’t fit the theme will reveal a hint to help you find the proper words.

The goal is to string together as many words as possible. Every letter on the grid gets used! Daily puzzles keep players coming back for more, making it a fun brain exercise and a popular online trend.

Strands can only be played on the New York Times website or on the NYT Games app.

Strands Hints For Today, April 10, 2026

The Strands’ answers to the theme of Game #768 are all terms related to being sneaky.

General Strands Hints For April 10

Without giving today’s Strands answers completely away, here are a few helpful hints for today to get you started:

  • Hint 1: Popular saying: Crazy ____ _ ___
  • Hint 2: An icy patch or a sly individual can be called this.
  • Hint 3: Someone skilled at knitting or painting.

Use these clues to narrow down your searches and increase your chances of solving Strands #768 for April 10, 2026.

Words to Help You Find Strand Hints for April 10, 2026

If you want help getting started without having today’s Strands answer given to you, there are a few starter words worth trying that will help you unlock some in-game hints. A few starter words that may help are:

  • CRAFT
  • LICK
  • LIED
  • OUTS
  • LIKE

Today’s Strands Answer For Game #768 On April 10, 2026

If you’ve tried everything, including using the above hints, and are still stuck, you can find the answers to today’s Strands below. Check out the answers for past puzzles here.

  • WILY
  • SLICK
  • DEVIOUS
  • CRAFTY
  • CANNY
  • SHREWD
  • CUNNING
  • SPANAGRAM: LIKEAFOX

If you solved today’s Strands without our answers, congratulations! If not, take note of the best tips above for tomorrow’s puzzle, and check back daily for more hints.

When Is The Next Strands Game After #768?

All New York Times games are updated daily at 12 am or Midnight in your respective time zone. Information about the next game is often available in the hours leading up to it, giving players a heads-up. The game on April 11 will be Strands Game #769.

What Is Strands?

Strands is a daily word search puzzle game created by The New York Times. Launched in 2024, Strands challenges players to discover a common theme among words in a 6×8 letter grid. The game’s original concept was developed by Juliette Seive, a research director on The New York Times Games team, with the aim of replicating Wordle’s success. Tracy Bennett serves as the puzzle editor for Strands, overseeing the creation and curation of daily puzzles since its launch.

For even more Insider Gaming delivered straight to your inbox, sign up for our weekly newsletter.
